547.name: J'rel Square
547.description: Cobblestones extend away from you to the edges of a large square. Two story buildings of subtle architecture fringe the area and artfully placed trees sprout from planters cut into the walkway. Older residents hurry about busily and a few students chat and debate amongst themselves. Your surroundings radiate a unique combination of old world simplicity and advanced technical skill. Kendal goes west.

npcs.name: J'relian man
npcs.description: The handsome J'relian man looks, like most those around him, like a

npcs.name: Student
npcs.description: are less than enthusiastic at the time.  Looks like this one is late for

npcs.name: Pendari F'daan
npcs.description: the Borash of J'rel.  Much nicer looking than that Grelore fellow.  Both of his ears are studded four times over with studs of some red-orange jewel.

549.name: Pud's Tavern
549.description: Parting a heavy curtain, a din of merrymaking assaults you. The air is thick with Poonk smoke and the music coming from the rickety piano in the corner. Mismatched bar stools are occupied by locals, some unconcious, and the bar itself is varnished and shined to a high gloss. A few tables are occupied by couples and town officials.

npcs.name: Half-conscious miner
npcs.description: stares meaningfully through his shotglass, mouth working drunkenly.

npcs.name: Pud Jessneth
npcs.description: Pud here lived in the stereotypical 'weird' family.  Every single one of his family was taught in the ways of bartending, male and female...odd, no?

551.name: Market Way
551.description: Colorful awnings overhang the entrances to shops along this, the most travelled street in town. The buildings are densely packed all around and down alleyways and the upper floors here are occupied by apartments and numerous rooms for rent.

npcs.name: Harker
npcs.description: I bet his produce lasts twice as long on the shelf just to make him proud.

553.name: Market Way
553.description: Store fronts display their wares and hawkers peddle useless trinkets on J'rel's market street. Two story buildings rise on either side of the road, their architecture a strange mix of human ruggedness and elven style. A soft sea- breeze blows from the harbor to the east. Market and Landing The gentle breaking of waves becomes clear as you come to the end of Market Way at the shoreline. An imposing boathouse pushes away to the east and the cobblestones of Landing Way stretch to the north along the beachfront and up a rocky grade to the south.

npcs.name: Child
npcs.description: which are impenetrable to anyone over 5.

npcs.name: Half-elf constable
npcs.description: up for constable training right now!  They even allow spell casters and thieves to train.  J'rel seems to hold diversity as that which will keep them

554.name: First Bank of J'rel
554.description: The bank seems like many others you've seen except for the conspicuous lack of a bank teller of any sort. A line of J'relians leads up to what appears to be a large metallic contraption that dominates the north wall as it whirs and buzzes. People seem quite happy conducting their business with it.

npcs.name: Bank machine
npcs.description: at its base reads, "Kindly keep your weapons away from the machine.  Its self-preservative response has no way of recognizing a friendly blade

npcs.name: Sub-inspector
npcs.description: up for constable training right now!  They even allow spell casters and thieves to train.  J'rel seems to hold diversity as that which will keep them

555.name: Market and Landing
555.description: The gentle breaking of waves becomes clear as you come to the end of Market Way at the shoreline. An imposing boathouse pushes away to the east and the cobblestones of Landing Way stretch to the north along the beachfront and up a rocky grade to the south.

npcs.name: J'relian woman
npcs.description: from a long line of half-elves like herself.

npcs.name: Pensive musician
npcs.description: seemingly lost in an internal world.  Small dextrous hands strum chords both uneven and beautiful, nothing like you've heard before.

557.name: On a Beach
557.description: Foamy water rolls smoothly up the fine white sand of this narrow beach and retreats back into Decaren Bay. Children splash happily in the day and couples stroll aimlessly at night, neither taking further notice of the seaward view that you now drink in willingly. The beach moves along to the north and is imposed upon by a boathouse to the south.

558.name: Beach's End
558.description: The beach peters to a halt just to the north as rockiness rises and stretches up and far away into a sea cliff. It wraps out and to the east in a gentle green- edged curve that ends at the mouth of the bay. A stone's throw from shore, a striking statue appears to stand atop the waves at high tide.

560.name: Boathouse
560.description: Bulky moorings and a curved ceiling thirty feet above boast of a facility capable of producing large ships. Currently a few smaller vessels of pleasure or trade are in various conditions of repair and construction and neat stacks of raw shipbuilding material lie in wait. Gulls line the rafters, scanning for easy meals. Docks stretch from the open end of the boathouse to the east.

npcs.name: Shendra Lan'thir
npcs.description: on her infamous luukir-skin boats.  Along with simple boats, Shendra also stocks some items you might need for a long nautical voyage.

561.name: Landing Way
561.description: The grade of the road from the north elevates it above the waves breaking against what is now a short rocky cliff. The path levels here and continues to the south with an unfettered view across the bay. Rooftops of shorter structures reflect natural light warmly and echoes of activity below make their way to your ears.

npcs.name: Thalia
npcs.description: A middle-aged human woman with long, wavy auburn hair that she usually ties back with a colorful silk scarf. Her eyes are a deep shade of green, and her face is adorned with faint laugh lines.

562.name: Landing's End
562.description: Roads rise and meet here at the inviting doors of a fine looking establishment. Ornate, but weathered, it projects from the cliff edge out into space supported by heavily reinforced beam work. Down to the west, the road moves toward a mammoth stone construction that cannot help but demand your attention.

npcs.name: Child
npcs.description: which are impenetrable to anyone over 5.

npcs.name: Half-elf constable
npcs.description: up for constable training right now!  They even allow spell casters and thieves to train.  J'rel seems to hold diversity as that which will keep them

563.name: Malik B's Suites
563.description: This once broken down inn has been remodeled into a hotel of respectable quality. A large plate-glass window overlooks the entire expanse of Decaren Bay, the peninsula that encloses it about a mile to the east. An assortment of traders and seamen mill about the lobby as you saunter up to the reception desk and ring a small brass bell.

npcs.name: Malik B'relna
npcs.description: was resolved to be the best innkeeper ever known.  He's refurbished the entire inn at no extra cost to the customer.

564.name: Lost and Found
564.description: Various articles of equipment and clothing left behind in tennants rooms are stored here, to help the needy. Though the armor and weaponry is in less than impressive condition, each piece has two very important qualities - they are here, and they are free.

566.name: University Way
566.description: The roadway moves past a broad and imposing stone structure to the north. Built like so many castles you've failed to raid, its sweeping buttresses, high- placed windows and stonework are all arranged into patterns that direct the eye toward a wide and central spire. An arch at its base straddles a heavily trafficked entryway that joins the road between twin stone obelisks.

npcs.name: J'relian man
npcs.description: The handsome J'relian man looks, like most those around him, like a

npcs.name: J'relian woman
npcs.description: from a long line of half-elves like herself.

npcs.name: Student
npcs.description: are less than enthusiastic at the time.  Looks like this one is late for

583.name: River and University
583.description: Cobblestones come to a corner in the shadow of taller structures, dense with windows. Locals filter in and out using some kind of keyless unlocking mechanism on panels near the first-floor doors. Many bearing books push by you busily while older residents nod politely as they pass. A pair of simple bronze lamp posts mark an exit from town to the south.

npcs.name: J'relian man
npcs.description: The handsome J'relian man looks, like most those around him, like a

586.name: Constables' Station
586.description: The heads of several large constables turn your direction as you enter their bastion of justice. The walls are made of thick stone, coated with cement. A single officer stands by a large door leading to the east, obviously the chief's office. To the north and south cell blocks lead off, gates blocking your entry. The jail cells are surprisingly empty, however.

npcs.name: Head constable
npcs.description: up for constable training right now!  They even allow spell casters and thieves to train.  J'rel seems to hold diversity as that which will keep them

587.name: The Chief's Office
587.description: Dense smoke and vital paperwork fill this room with a purposeful air. Documents all around protrude from filing cabinets and lay coffee-stained on tables. Curled sketches of sinister mugs are pinned several deep on bulletin boards and confiscated evidence has been shoved into piles. In the midst of it all is an utterly spotless desk bearing only a picture frame.

npcs.name: Grag Hol-Raal
npcs.description: A giant beast of an officer, the Chief looks to be of half-orcish descent. However he came by a half-elven last name, only his wife knows..

611.name: A Muddy Road
611.description: The back sides of the buildings of J'rel seem none too friendly to the unkempt path forcing its way through and into town. Its course continues roughly away to the northwest and over a rise in the landscape that blocks your view. Toward the river a smaller trail dips into a depression in the land. A smelly depression.

613.name: Atop a Rise
613.description: Surmounting the rise, the muddy brown ribbon of the path is visible across the green expanse of plain sweeping north and west of you before disappearing into a distant mist. Dense forest crowds the bank of the Frektal river to the south and a rocky peak rises from the mist at the limits of your vision across the plain.

615.name: Infirmary
615.description: Walking down a short hallway and turning a corner, you come upon a large predominantly white room. The back wall is covered by an ornate wooden cabinet filled with bottles of various size and color. Comfy looking cots line the sterile walls, some occupied by ill J'relians. A round skylight imparts a measure of cheer to this sombre place.

npcs.name: Doctor Laandir
npcs.description: Doctor Laandir has his understudy, Nurse Sevvel, attend to the wounded. I'm sure, in the interest of J'rel's future, the doctor would oblige to a

npcs.name: Nurse Sevvel
npcs.description: day is this exceptionally cute nurse.  Even while dressed in the plainest nurse's outfit and blood apron could she win a beauty contest.

616.name: Phrr'dens Pharmeceuticals
616.description: Neatly arranged shelves display all manner of potions and tonics in artfully blown glass bottles, while racks to either side of the counter are stacked high with neatly rolled scrolls. A rainbow of different colored pills fill tiny trays in a large glass cabinet.

npcs.name: Old man Phrr'den
npcs.description: merrily and even stopping to jump and click his heels from time to time. You wonder just how well medicated he keeps himself.

550.name: Tinker's Loft
550.description: Striding up the staircase, a distinctly mechanical clatter grows louder. The room seems to be both an apartment and shop as living accomodations take up the room not occupied by strange conglomerations of rattling gears, belts, and pumps. Tools, small and large, hang from a board.

npcs.name: Senrath Festa-Raal
npcs.description: you've seen -- and with the last name "Festa-Raal" you begin to wonder. While this aged ancestor looks incredibly frail, you're surprised at his

559.name: A Long Wooden Launch
559.description: A small grassy clearing is bare but for a long wooden boardwalk that moves out to the edge of the low sea cliff, and out 20 feet beyond over the breaking waves. The wind blusters from seaward, moist with sea mist.

npcs.name: Bruno von Zuffenhausenschaftig
npcs.description: slim to his slight figure.  Poking above the high collar is a head just like that of a river trout, thin and gaunt.

598.name: A Quaint Kitchen
598.description: A bulky iron stove contains a roaring cooking fire and heats the kitchen uncomfortably. Carefully labelled spice jars line shelves and an eating table fit for two sits at the middle. Makes you want a big ol' slice of pie, being here.
